milestones.name: Core Simulator
milestones.goal: A playable, deterministic language-evolution simulator ‚Äî seeded world generation, sound-change rules, territory expansion, autonomous drift/spread/fracture, and a mutual intelligibility matrix.

tasks.description: Mulberry32 seeded RNG + deterministic hash for autonomous replay (`rng.ts`)
Phoneme inventory, syllable template, and 32-concept lexicon generation (`lexicon.ts`)
Phone table, 10 sound-change rules, collision/homophone detection (`phonology.ts`)
Mutual intelligibility matrix via normalised edit distance (`intelligibility.ts`)
Branch family tree layout and per-branch colour generation (`tree.ts`)
Terrain map, adjacency, and passable-component detection (`geography.ts`)
World/state initialisation (`world.ts`)
Generation resolution: drift ‚Üí spread ‚Üí fracture ‚Üí repool (`generation.ts`)

Research spike: rule-set erosion/renewal balance ‚Äî diagnosed why the purely-reductive `RULES` ossify a branch after a few generations, surveyed real diachronic renewal mechanisms (epenthesis, vowel breaking/diphthongisation, monophthongisation, vowel shortening), and produced a build-ready contract for 1ENG.12 (`docs/spikes/1eng-11-erosion-renewal.md`)

Erosion/renewal mechanism ‚Äî widened the phonology transducer to a 1‚ÜíN segment model, added real diphthong/long-vowel phones (seeded into starting inventories too), and added `epenth`/`break`/`paragoge` (renewal) plus `smooth`/`shorten` (erosion of the new structure) rules. Testing during implementation found the spike's original mid-vowel-conditioned `break` couldn't bootstrap from a fully-eroded lexicon (~97% of turns still ossified); corrected to an unconditioned `break` + new `paragoge` rule, which together guarantee every word has a live renewal move ‚Äî verified at 0 ossified turns across 150-turn/multi-seed end-to-end sweeps (`docs/spikes/1eng-11-erosion-renewal.md`, `phonology.ts`)
Fracture: children now diverge from the parent at the moment of fracture via a seeded birth drift step against the post-split owner map, instead of starting as exact lexicon copies (`generation.ts`)

Fracture reworked to continue the parent lineage on its largest surviving component (ties broken by lowest region id) instead of retiring the parent and minting a fresh branch per component; only the other component(s) spin off as new siblings. Added a divergence-threshold rename mechanic: every branch accrues a flat chain of drift anchors (`Anchor`), and a new `naming.ts` module renders them at display time via an event-density-aware perspective-collapse into Old/Middle/Late era names and `Proto-<blend>` names for shared ancestors of genuinely diverged descendants ‚Äî bare stems for living tips. Branch names are now generated phonotactically from each branch's own inventory (`naming.ts` `genStem`) instead of drawn from a static pool. Surfaced and fixed a latent bug in `isLeaf`/`leavesOf` (previously "childless", which silently broke once a still-territory-owning branch could also have children) and a `root.name` placeholder ("Proto") colliding with the new Proto- naming vocabulary (`generation.ts`, `naming.ts`, `tree.ts`, `world.ts`)

Language-shift/assimilation death ‚Äî a much smaller branch bordering (via a passable edge) a near-identical, much larger neighbour (intelligibility above a cutoff, size ratio below a threshold) sustained over several turns has its territory absorbed into that neighbour and becomes a dead ancestor. Closes a gap surfaced after 1ENG.10 shipped: nothing in the engine previously emptied a branch's territory, so the Old/Middle/Late/Proto- era-naming payoff was unreachable in play ‚Äî this makes it reachable via a real historical death pattern (Cornish, Manx) rather than territory conquest, keeping clear of 2GEO.4's planned neighbour-contact/borrowing scope. New `geography.ts` `neighborsOf`/`dominantAssimilator` helpers, a new turn-loop step in `generation.ts` (after spread, before fracture), and a UI warning mirroring the existing fracture warning (`game.svelte.ts`, `+page.svelte`)
Reactive game state singleton (Svelte 5 runes) (`game.svelte.ts`)
Map, family tree, intelligibility matrix, word table, change list, history panels
Economy config panel for tuning pool/growth/overhead/cost settings
Main route wiring all components together (`+page.svelte`)

Compensatory lengthening ‚Äî split into `compleng` (medial coda in a cluster, `V _ C`, e.g. kast‚ÜíkaÀêt) and `complengFinal` (word-final coda, `V _ #`, e.g. tas‚ÜítaÀê), mirroring the epenth/paragoge medial/final split; both fire on the coda and use a new `Rule.lengthensPrev` flag so `applyRuleToWord` reaches back and lengthens the vowel it already emitted ‚Äî the 1ENG.11 spike ¬ß8 claim that `Seg[]` already supported this was inaccurate (corrected in the spike doc)

Design spike: syntax-conditioned sound change ‚Äî many real sound changes are conditioned by the preceding/following *word*, not just the preceding/following phoneme (sandhi, cross-word assimilation/liaison); the engine currently has no representation of word order or utterance-level context at all (each lexicon entry is an isolated concept‚Üíword mapping). Needs its own spike to define a minimal syntax/adjacency model before any cross-word rule can be built

Design spike: morphological renewal (agreement/tense) ‚Äî noun-verb agreement and grammatical tense marking are a major real-world source of the vocabulary/sound variety that keeps a language's phonology alive (paradigms, inflectional affixes); named but explicitly out-of-scope in the 1ENG.11 spike (¬ß8) as belonging with 2GEO.4/borrowing. Needs a spike to define a minimal inflectional-paradigm model before implementation

Research spike: survey Zompist conlang tools ([SCA](https://www.zompist.com/sca2.html), [Gen](https://www.zompist.com/gen.html), [Phono](https://www.zompist.com/phono.html), [GGG](https://www.zompist.com/ggg.html), [GTG](https://www.zompist.com/gtg.html), [MG](https://www.zompist.com/mg.html)) for mechanisms and approaches applicable to our own engine ‚Äî sound-change rule notation and batch application (SCA), procedural word/name generation (Gen), phonology description conventions (Phono), and general conlanging methodology (GGG, GTG, MG); produce a build-ready contract for 1ENG.17 identifying which findings are worth adopting
Implement chosen improvements from the Zompist tools survey *(placeholder ‚Äî depends on 1ENG.16)*

milestones.name: Depth & Legibility
milestones.goal: Make geography causally shape language change, give sound-change choices real stakes, add a diegetic evolving-glyph writing system, and make every player-facing decision legible ‚Äî starting with an onboarding pass informed by all of the above.

tasks.description: Design spike: terrain‚Üísound-change bias ruleset ‚Äî split into a social-geography contact/isolation axis (sound change) and a physical-geography terrain axis (semantic salience), with a full implementation contract for 2GEO.2 and 2GEO.3 (`docs/spikes/2geo-1-terrain-sound-change.md`)
Implement terrain-biased rule weighting in `phonology.ts` ‚Äî contract specified in the 2GEO.1 spike

Implement biome-driven vocabulary resistance ‚Äî terrain-salient concepts (`salienceRetention` in `lexicon.ts`) drift/replace more slowly, gating word-level drift in `applyRuleToLex` (`phonology.ts`) ‚Äî contract specified in the 2GEO.1 spike, Axis B minimum scope (iii)

Design spike: neighbour contact/borrowing mechanic ‚Äî bordering branches converge via borrowing, the one convergent force in an otherwise all-divergent turn loop (drift, fracture and assimilation-death all pull branches apart or remove one). Specifies a directional per-ordered-pair mechanic: eligibility gated on the lender's terrain-salient concepts (`salienceRetention`/`dominantTerrain` ‚Äî the borrowable subset of an otherwise borrowing-resistant basic list, per Tadmor/WOLD), selection of the most-divergent eligible concept, a pair-local contact throttle (passable A‚ÄìB edges as a share of A's border), and a contact-graded outcome (faithful whole-copy at high contact, one-step adaptation at low contact ‚Äî contact proxying bilingual proficiency, per Thomason & Kaufman). Salient concepts thus resist drift *and* attract borrowing (the real Wanderwort profile). Full build-ready contract for 2GEO.5, following the 2GEO.1 pattern (`docs/spikes/2geo-4-neighbour-contact-borrowing.md`)

Implement the neighbour-borrowing mechanic ‚Äî new `borrowing.ts` (`resolveBorrow`, `BORROW_RATE`, `BORROW_FAITHFUL_CUT`), `pairContact` in `geography.ts`, `formSimilarity` factored out of `intelligibility.ts`, `borrowableConcepts`/`stepToward` beside the salience helpers, and a new turn-loop step 3.5 in `generation.ts` (after spread, before assimilation). No new randomness beyond a fresh `hashRand` salt; seeded replay preserved ‚Äî contract specified in the 2GEO.4 spike

Design spike: homophone-collision resolution mechanic ‚Äî how a real semantic collision between two concepts (`homophoneForms`/`collisionPairs` in `phonology.ts`, currently only previewed before a player picks a rule) gets resolved once it actually lands during drift ‚Äî candidates include a forced disambiguating follow-up change, a tolerated real homophone surfaced in UI/narrative, a borrowed synonym, or compounding ‚Äî informed by real linguistics on homophone avoidance/tolerance
Design spike: rule-choice stakes mechanic (resource trade-offs vs directional goals vs prerequisite chains)
Implement chosen rule-stakes mechanic
Design spike: glyph mutation ruleset ‚Äî shape-drift grammar + phoneme‚Üíglyph reassignment rules, referencing real script lineages (e.g. Phoenician ‚Üí Greek ‚Üí Etruscan ‚Üí Latin)
Implement per-generation glyph shape drift (independent stylistic mutation)
Implement phoneme‚Üíglyph reassignment logic, tied to phone split/merge/deletion from phonology rules
Build glyph rendering component (branch-level script display)
Implement chosen homophone-collision resolution mechanic
UI completeness audit across all components ‚Äî existing panels plus new biome/stakes/glyph/lexicon data ‚Äî verify every player-facing decision has a legible data source
Build onboarding ‚Äî inline explainers, full tutorial mode, and a UI layout rethink, informed by the audit findings

milestones.name: Persistence & Sharing
milestones.goal: Survive a page refresh and let players show their results to someone else. Deferred out of Milestone 2 to keep that milestone focused purely on simulation depth and legibility ‚Äî stubbed here so the intent isn't lost.

tasks.description: Local persistence ‚Äî save/load a session via `localStorage`
Shareable output ‚Äî export/share a family tree or result (image, link, or data export)
